title: JIT dispatch integration — interpreter to native transitions
purpose: The interpreter must detect when a function has compiled native code and dispatch to it instead of interpreting bytecode. Conversely, compiled code must be able to call back into the interpreter for functions that haven't been compiled yet.
task: Implement the interpreter-to-native and native-to-interpreter transition mechanisms.

requirements.title: Interpreter dispatch to native code
requirements.spec: When the interpreter encounters a call to a JIT-compiled function, dispatch to the native code instead of interpreting.
acceptance: Call dispatch checks JitCache before interpreting
If native code exists, call the function pointer directly
Process state (registers, stack) is consistent between interpreter and native code
Test: interpret function A which calls JIT-compiled function B — correct result

requirements.title: Native to interpreter fallback
requirements.spec: Compiled code calls back to the interpreter for functions not yet compiled.
acceptance: Runtime helper: jit_call_interpreted(process, module, function, arity, args)
Compiled code uses this for cross-module calls to non-compiled functions
Return value is passed back to compiled code correctly
Test: JIT-compiled function calls interpreted function — correct result

requirements.title: Reduction counting in compiled code
requirements.spec: Compiled code must decrement the reduction counter and yield when exhausted.
acceptance: Each function entry in compiled code decrements the reduction counter
When counter reaches 0, compiled code returns to the scheduler via a yield point
Process state is saved correctly at yield
Test: compiled tight loop yields after reduction budget exhausted

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement inline caching for call targets — simple cache lookup only
SHALL NOT optimize cross-module calls — each is a full dispatch
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
